text: In this video, we would like to present an intra-anal fistulotomy with marsupialization. We would like to start by reminding you of Park's classification of most common fistulas. And also the Goodsall's rule that states if the external opening is anterior to the line, the fistula usually runs directly into the anal canal. And if the external opening is posterior to the line, the fistula usually curves to the posterior midline of the canal.

A fifty-year-old gentleman presented at our clinic complaining of constant secretion through anus. From his previous medical history he had intersphincteric fistula that was treated several times. MRI was performed. High intersphincteric fistula was detected.

The surgeon identifies the internal opening of the fistula at the level of the dentate line in the posterior midline, noting the absence of an external opening. A schematic representation of the fistula tract is shown, classified as an intersphincteric fistula with a rectal opening and no perineal opening according to Park's classification.

The patient is positioned in a prone jackknife position with buttocks taped aside. Retractors are placed to expose the surgical field. A probe is inserted through the internal opening to explore the blind high intersphincteric fistula tract, revealing several milliliters of purulent discharge. A swab is taken for microbiological analysis.
The surgeon performs a phthalotomy using monopolar electrocautery, with a probe placed from the internal opening to the end of the blind intersphincteric fistula tract.
The surgeon performs curettage of the open fistula tract, followed by marsupialization, which involves suturing the edges of the fistula tract to the surrounding skin to promote healing.
The surgeon inserts a hemostatic sponge into the surgical site to control bleeding within the fistula tract.

Here you can see patient two months after operation. As the patient did not complain of fecal incontinence either before or after operation, the anorectal manometry was not indicated and not performed.
Wexner's score before and after operation was zero. Thank you.
